message:
chore(beads): mark observability metrics beads as closed

- bd-397f0: Feature: Observability Metrics - closed (core metrics.go and TUI panel exist)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -406,7 +406,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-30nv1","title":"Implement keystroke_echo probe method","description":"Implement keystroke_echo probe method.

## Method

Send invisible character, check for cursor/output response.

## Implementation

```go
func probeKeystrokeEcho(session string, pane int, timeout time.Duration) ProbeResult {
    // 1. Capture baseline (include cursor position if possible)
    baseline := capturePaneContent(session, pane)
    
    // 2. Send null byte (invisible, non-disruptive)
    exec.Command("/usr/bin/tmux", "send-keys", "-t", target, "\x00").Run()
    
    // 3. Wait for response (poll with short intervals)
    deadline := time.Now().Add(timeout)
    for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
        current := capturePaneContent(session, pane)
        if current != baseline {
            return ProbeResult{
                Responsive: true,
                LatencyMs: ...,
            }
        }
        time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)
    }
    
    // 4. No change detected
    return ProbeResult{Responsive: false}
}
```

## Considerations

- Null byte may not echo in all shells
- Alternative: Send ESC sequence that triggers cursor report
- Consider tmux capture-pane -p vs -pC (with cursor)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:32:57.558570422Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:44:38.018027499Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:44:38.017983126Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-30nv1","depends_on_id":"bd-1cu1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-30nv1","depends_on_id":"bd-58w0z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-30nv1","depends_on_id":"bd-ok7rj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-30o1y","title":"Unit Tests: ntm assign --clear assignment removal logic","description":"## Test Coverage
Unit tests for ntm assign --clear functionality.

## Test Cases

### Single Clear
- Clear assigned bead
- Verify assignment removed from store
- Verify file reservations released

### Batch Clear
- Clear multiple beads (comma-separated)
- Verify all cleared correctly
- Partial failure handling

### Clear by Pane
- Clear all assignments for pane 3
- Handle pane with no assignments

### Clear Failed
- Clear only failed assignments
- Leave working/completed untouched

### Error Handling
- Clear non-assigned bead -> error
- Clear completed bead without --force -> error
- Clear completed bead with --force -> success

### Edge Cases
- Clear already cleared assignment
- Clear during concurrent assignment

## Test File
tests/assign_clear_test.go","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:39:28.172854834Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:53:51.322102548Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:53:51.322054126Z","close_reason":"Added unit tests for assign --clear logic","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-30o1y","depends_on_id":"bd-2a5yf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-30ws","title":"Task: Implement SummarizeSession() function","description":"## Purpose
Generate concise, actionable summaries of agent session output at session end.

## Scope
- Implement SummarizeSession() in internal/summary/generator.go
- Extract key accomplishments, changes made, and pending work
- Parse structured output from agent sessions
- Support multiple summary formats (brief, detailed, handoff)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Summaries capture all significant changes
- File changes are listed with context
- Pending work items are extracted
- Error summary included if applicable
- Token count reasonable for injection into next session

## Technical Notes
- Consider using LLM for summarization if available
- Fallback to pattern-based extraction
- Integrate with Agent Mail for thread summaries","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:00:21.180715869Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T00:56:40.940638964Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T00:56:40.940593108Z","close_reason":"Implemented SummarizeSession in internal/summary and wired into CLI","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-30ws","depends_on_id":"bd-3dxk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-30xgf","title":"Task: Implement MetricsPanel component for observability dashboard","description":"# Task: Implement MetricsPanel for Dashboard

## Location
internal/tui/dashboard/panels/metrics.go

## Purpose
Real-time observability panel showing coverage map, redundancy score, findings velocity, and conflict density during and after ensemble runs.

## Panel Design
```
┌─ Metrics ─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Coverage: 65% (5/8 categories)                │
│ [A]██ [B]░░ [E]██ [F]█░ [G]██ [H]░░ [K]█░ [L]█│
│                                               │
│ Redundancy: 0.34 (acceptable)                 │
│ ├─ F1 ↔ E2: 0.23 (low)                        │
│ └─ K1 ↔ H2: 0.67 (high - review suggested)   │
│                                               │
│ Velocity: 2.3 findings/1k tokens              │
│ ├─ Top: F1 (3.1), E2 (2.8)                   │
│ └─ Low: K1 (1.2) ⚠                           │
│                                               │
│ Conflicts: 3 detected, 1 resolved             │
│ ├─ F1 ↔ E2: root cause theory                │
│ └─ K1 ↔ H2: scope disagreement               │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Implementation
```go
type MetricsPanel struct {
    coverage   *CoverageReport
    redundancy *RedundancyAnalysis
    velocity   *VelocityReport
    conflicts  *ConflictDensity
    expanded   map[string]bool  // toggle detail sections
    width      int
    height     int
}

func NewMetricsPanel() *MetricsPanel
func (p *MetricsPanel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (Panel, tea.Cmd)
func (p *MetricsPanel) View() string
func (p *MetricsPanel) renderCoverageMap() string
func (p *MetricsPanel) renderRedundancy() string
func (p *MetricsPanel) renderVelocity() string
func (p *MetricsPanel) renderConflicts() string
```

## Behavior
- Panel reads precomputed reports (no metric calculation in UI)
- Gracefully handles nil/partial metrics (shows "N/A")
- Stable ordering to avoid flicker

## Keyboard Shortcuts
- 'c' - Toggle coverage detail
- 'r' - Toggle redundancy detail
- 'v' - Toggle velocity detail
- 'x' - Toggle conflicts detail

## Logging Requirements
- Log metric values on update (rate-limited or only on change)
- Log threshold violations (high redundancy, low velocity)

## Tests
- Unit: render output with sample metrics (no panic, stable sections)
- Unit: nil/partial metrics handling

## Dependencies
- CoverageMap (bd-39lt4)
- RedundancyScore (bd-254do)
- FindingsVelocity (bd-14h3d)
- ConflictDensity (bd-2eblo)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T18:58:44.668844600Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:48:25.964639387Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-30xgf","depends_on_id":"bd-14h3d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-30xgf","depends_on_id":"bd-254do","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-30xgf","depends_on_id":"bd-2eblo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-30xgf","depends_on_id":"bd-397f0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-30xgf","depends_on_id":"bd-39lt4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-30xgf","depends_on_id":"bd-rylze","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-30zp4","title":"Task: REST checkpoint + rollback endpoints","description":"## Scope\nExpose checkpoints/rollback APIs for sessions.\n\n## Endpoints\n- /sessions/{name}/checkpoints (list/create)\n- /sessions/{name}/checkpoints/{id} (get/delete)\n- /sessions/{name}/checkpoints/{id}/restore\n- /sessions/{name}/checkpoints/{id}/verify\n- /sessions/{name}/checkpoints/{id}/export\n- /sessions/{name}/checkpoints/import\n- /sessions/{name}/rollback\n\n## Logging\n- Log checkpoint IDs, session, and restore outcomes.\n\n## Tests\n- Unit: validation + error codes.\n- Integration: create checkpoint -> delete -> verify.\n- Parity: REST responses match CLI checkpoint outputs.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Checkpoint lifecycle works via REST.\n- Rollback preserves existing semantics.\n","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralHawk","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:50:10.938953424Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T21:52:01.809920150Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T21:52:01.809363381Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-30zp4","depends_on_id":"bd-3fqg9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-30zp4","depends_on_id":"bd-yn7qe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31e1f","title":"Feature: Robot Diagnose Command (--robot-diagnose)","description":"## Overview

Add `ntm --robot-diagnose <session>` command that provides a single comprehensive
health check answering "what's wrong and how do I fix it?"

## Background

Currently diagnosing swarm health requires:
1. Loop through each pane
2. Capture and grep output for error patterns
3. Check process state
4. Interpret results
5. Decide on action

This is tedious and error-prone. A single diagnose command should do all of this.

## Proposed Interface

```bash
ntm --robot-diagnose myproject
```

## Proposed Output

```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-19T23:15:00Z",
  "overall_health": "degraded",  // "healthy", "degraded", "critical"
  "summary": {
    "total_panes": 16,
    "healthy": 12,
    "rate_limited": 2,
    "unresponsive": 1,
    "crashed": 1
  },
  "panes": {
    "healthy": [2, 3, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 11, 12, 13, 14, 16],
    "rate_limited": [4, 10],
    "unresponsive": [15],
    "crashed": [1]
  },
  "recommendations": [
    {
      "pane": 4,
      "status": "rate_limited",
      "action": "wait_or_caam",
      "reason": "Output contains 'You've hit your limit'",
      "reset_time": "4pm ET",  // parsed from output if available
      "auto_fixable": false
    },
    {
      "pane": 10,
      "status": "rate_limited",
      "action": "wait_or_caam",
      "reason": "Output contains 'You've hit your limit'",
      "auto_fixable": false
    },
    {
      "pane": 15,
      "status": "unresponsive",
      "action": "kill_restart",
      "reason": "No output for 312 seconds, process state: sleeping",
      "auto_fixable": true,
      "fix_command": "ntm --robot-restart-pane myproject 15"
    },
    {
      "pane": 1,
      "status": "crashed",
      "action": "restart_cc",
      "reason": "Shell prompt visible, no agent process",
      "auto_fixable": true,
      "fix_command": "ntm --robot-restart-pane myproject 1"
    }
  ],
  "auto_fix_available": true,
  "auto_fix_command": "ntm --robot-diagnose myproject --fix"
}
```

## Health Classification Logic

| Status | Detection Criteria |
|--------|-------------------|
| healthy | Agent process running, recent output, no error patterns |
| rate_limited | Output contains rate limit patterns |
| unresponsive | No output for >5min AND process running |
| crashed | No agent process OR zombie process |
| unknown | Cannot determine (provide raw data) |

## Flags

- `--fix`: Automatically fix what can be fixed (crashed/unresponsive, not rate limits)
- `--json`: (default) JSON output
- `--brief`: Just summary, no per-pane details
- `--pane=N`: Diagnose specific pane only

## Why This Matters

Reduces "what's wrong?" from a 5-minute manual inspection to a 1-second command.
The recommendations with fix commands enable scripted recovery.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:16:03.415109604Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:18:25.661480205Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:18:25.660292387Z","close_reason":"Implemented --robot-diagnose command with health classification, recommendations, and auto-fix support. Created internal/robot/diagnose.go and added flags to root.go.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31e1f","depends_on_id":"bd-1g9wv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-31e1f","depends_on_id":"bd-258a1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31hj","title":"Unit Tests: Session summarization logic","description":"## Comprehensive Unit Test Suite for Session Summarization

### Test Coverage Required
1. **AccomplishmentExtractor tests** (internal/summary/extract_test.go)
   - File change detection
   - Commit identification
   - Test result parsing
   - Decision extraction

2. **SummaryGenerator tests** (internal/summary/generate_test.go)
   - Per-agent summary generation
   - Statistics calculation
   - Remaining work identification
   - Markdown formatting

3. **SummaryStorage tests** (internal/summary/store_test.go)
   - JSON serialization
   - Markdown export
   - Index maintenance

### Test Data
- Sample scrollbacks with various activities
- Git commits, test output, discussions
- Edge cases: empty session, no changes

### Logging
t.Logf("SUMMARY_TEST: %s | Agents=%d | Commits=%d | TestResults=%d", testName, agents, commits, tests)","notes":"Added comprehensive unit tests for session summarization logic. Tests cover: section extraction (extractSectionItems), key action extraction (extractKeyActions), change highlights (extractChangeHighlights), error line extraction (extractErrorLines), pending inline extraction (extractPendingInline), file change extraction (extractFileChanges, extractPathsFromLine, inferFileAction), JSON parsing (parseStructuredJSON, extractJSONBlocks), formatting (formatBrief, formatDetailed), handoff building (buildHandoffSummary), token truncation (truncateToTokens, truncateAtRuneBoundary), helper functions (appendUnique, appendUniqueList, firstNonEmpty), multi-agent output aggregation, edge cases, and benchmarks. Total: 89 test runs all passing.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:17:03.409248696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T20:59:32.704145836Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T20:59:32.704044044Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31hj","depends_on_id":"bd-3dxk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
